key_insight: WEST is an open-source, highly scalable excited-state simulation code that unifies GW, BSE, TDDFT, and quantum defect embedding without explicitly computing virtual states.
engineering_relevance: It enables predictive excited-state calculations for very large systems on thousands of GPUs, supporting materials discovery, spectroscopy, and high-fidelity datasets for downstream AI workflows.
method: Combines DFT and density-matrix perturbation theory with low-rank dielectric screening and exact exchange.
Uses localization techniques and a common algorithmic framework to avoid explicit virtual-state calculations.
Implements hierarchical parallelization plus GPU acceleration for near-ideal strong scaling.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports representative applications and scaling results, including simulations of systems with more than a thousand atoms and near-ideal strong scaling to thousands of GPUs.
watch_item: Performance and usability will still depend on system-specific convergence, workflow interoperability, and the practical cost of large-scale GPU resources.
paper_title: The WEST code for large-scale excited-state materials simulations
